messages.content:
You will be given the current section title and the current page_text.
    Your job is to check if the current section starts in the beginning of the given page_text.
    If there are other contents before the current section title,
    then the current section does not start in the beginning of the given page_text.
    If the current section title is the first content in the given page_text,
    then the current section starts in the beginning of the given page_text.

    Note: do fuzzy matching, ignore any space inconsistency in the page_text.

    The given section title is 媒体声音.
    The given page_text is 66媒体声音
白鲸出海、独立出海联合体、扬帆出海
.

    reply format:
    {
        "start_begin": "yes or no" (yes if the section starts in the beginning of the page_text, no otherwise)
    }
    Directly return the final JSON structure. Do not output anything else.

Do not include any thinking field, explanation, or reasoning. Return only the final requested JSON or text.

message.reasoning_content:
The user wants to check if the section title "媒体声音" starts at the beginning of the page_text "66媒体声音\n白鲸出海、独立出海联合体、扬帆出海\n.".
The page_text starts with "66媒体声音".
The section title is "媒体声音".
There is a "66" before "媒体声音".
So the section title is not the first content. It has "66" before it.
Therefore, it does not start at the beginning.
Wait, "66" might be a page number. The prompt says: "If there are other contents before the current section title, then the current section does not start in the beginning of the given page_text."
So "66" is other content.
Thus, start_begin should be "no".
Let's double check. "66媒体声音" -> "66" is before "媒体声音".
So "no".
Output JSON: {"start_begin": "no"}
Done. 
Check format: 
{
    "start_begin": "no"
}
Matches requirement.
Proceed. 
No extra text.
Done. 
Output matches exactly. 
Final answer: {"start_begin": "no"}
